Um, I'm not sure if I've shared this, um, uh, I'm not sure if I've, uh, shared this story with you guys yet or not, but, uh, it's, it's kind of a, it's a true story. There's, um, there's this, uh, this guy that I follow his name is Brooks Kubic. Uh, he, he runs a site called dinosaur training.com. It's all this like big weightlifting type stuff, right? I'm really into it. And, um, when I was in high school, I bought one of his books called dinosaur strength training. It's amazing. One of the best books I've ever, I've ever read on, on, um, on lifting weights and just it was awesome. So I read it and became a huge fan of this guy and he had all these DVDs he was selling back then or excuse me, they were, uh, they were videos VHS is back then and, uh, they were like $50 a piece and there was five or six of them. And I was like, oh, it's like 300 bucks. I'm like, there's no way I'm going to be able to afford that. Then we fast forward like five or six years later. I had my business, I had a little extra money. And so of course, first thing I wanted is I wanted Brooks's, uh, uh, now DVDs and same thing, they were like 50 bucks a piece. And so I went and bought them all and I was telling my friends about them. I thought like, I was just so excited to get them. And then two of my friends were actually at my house when they showed up. And so we put the DVDs in because they wanted to see it. They're like, man, you've been talking about this guy forever. Let's check it out. So I put the first DVD in, I pushed play and we started watching it and like the screen's completely black. You can't see anything. And I'm like, what's, what's going on? Like, you know, it's a DVD broken. And all of a sudden you hear, uh, this female voice saying, huh, and I can't see you. And then you hear him say, we got to take the lens cap off, dear. And he, all of a sudden you see like his hand, take the lens cap off the camera and he backs up, sits down on the bench and starts doing like a bench press or something. And then he records him, you know, showing how to do these, these, uh, these way, lifting things. And I remember my friends are laughing at me so hard and said, man, you totally got scammed. This guy sends you, you know, these DVDs. He filmed with his like his own camera in his backyard and, and, uh, and they were laughing at me and we turned it off because I was kind of embarrassed. Anyway, my friends that night, they left obviously. And, um, after they left, I went and put the DVDs back in and I watched them. And the DVDs were amazing. Like the information that I got from them, like change my, my perspective, my understanding of, of how all these things work. And I remember for me as a consumer, I didn't care what it looked like. I just wanted to know what Brooks knew. Like he's this guy who I respected, who had a different angle, who was prolific and, uh, and exciting. And I wanted to know exactly what he knew and it was worth every penny, uh, the investment for me. Okay. And that's how it works with your audience. You guys, like they're trying to connect with you. They're not trying to connect with Hollywood. If they were, they would go and they would buy, you know, dummy's guide to whatever. Like they're going to get a professionally polished product, but they bonded to you. Okay. Um, they're bonding to you and your, your personality and your character. And that's what's key is, is the information that comes from you. The more raw and authentic it is, the better it's going to be. Okay. And so, uh, it's, I hope that, I hope that helps. Okay. So if you guys go out into your space and start finding blogs and competitors and see what their hooks are and their angles. If you do that, I promise you you're going to find, you're going to find one that's going to resonate with what your message is. And you kind of tweak it. And obviously you're not copying it, but tweak it and make it your own. And as soon as you do that, then boom, you can go out and use that as your, as your hook for your, for your free product. Okay. All right. Let me come back here. All right. So find a good hook, find competitors and you tell your story. So I'm going to dive a little deeper into finding your good hook. So when you're finding a hook, the first thing I always look for is, and this is where I guarantee a lot of you guys are going to get this wrong is you got to find, you got to create a hard offer, not a soft offer. Okay. So I'm probably saying what's the hard, what's this hard offer, soft offer. So, like a soft offer is like, Hey, you're going to eat this food and you'll feel better. Okay. It's kind of soft. Like, Oh, I'll feel better. Like, what does that mean? Okay. Hard offer is eat this food and you'll lose 13 pounds. Okay. Even harder offers eat this food and you lose 13 pounds in like seven days. Okay. Specifics, um, or what make things, uh, not so much specifics, but, but tying it to either time or money or both or a specific number, like that's how you make something more of a hard offer. Okay. Um, if you can tie it, something like that, it becomes so much more powerful. That's why, you know, in the, the personal development space is really hard. I, every time I try to sell something that in that niche, it's hard because, because you, mostly the offers are soft. Like how to feel happier, how to, how to, um, you know, how to, I don't even know, like all the hooks, you know, um, and it's hard. Like the people that are in there, they've got audience that have come to them, but it's, it's different. They don't suck somebody as fast with a soft offer. As soon as you bring it out, a hard offer. Um, you know, like, uh, one of my friends is in, uh, I wonder if I can find his site real quick for you. Um, he's in the stock trading industry. Um, Preston James is his name. He's got this, uh, the one stock trick. He's got a direct MLP talks about one stock trick. It's right like 90, um, 7% of the time. Wonder if I can find the headline from this thing. That'd be cool if I could. Yeah. This is his headline. This magic little known piece of news makes a stock go up or down in the next three months, falling its release 86% of the time. Boom. See, that's a hard offer. Okay. If I knew what that one little thing was, then at 86% of the time, I'm going to make money. What is that thing? And, and, and hint, it's not earnings. Oh crap. I thought it was going to be earnings. What is that one piece? Okay. These guys have literally made a over $10 million a year business off of this little headline right here. They mail out these direct mail pieces talking about this and they're killing it. Um, but it's, see, it's a tangible thing. It's like you tie it to, to time, percentages, specific numbers, things like that is what really helps it, um, to become specific. Okay. So with what it is you're selling, how can you make it a hard offer? So instead of, let's say you're doing like, um, natural food instead of like, how to make natural food. Yeah. How do you make that hard? How do you make natural food that'll help your kids stay thin? Okay. Well, how do you make natural food that help your kids lose, um, lose a pound a week. Okay. Um, you see how I'm doing that guys? You got to figure out how to, how to tweak it. So it's, so it's more hard because if you do that, again, people are going to be excited and your, your, your ability to sell us will become so much easier when you're, uh, when you're, you're tied to something that's, that's hard like that. Okay. So that's the first thing. Make sure you have a hard offer, not a soft offer. The next thing is you have to make it a tangible thing, something, something that's, that's, um, you know, uh, something that they, they can see. Okay. Losing a specific weight or specific, um, percentage or whatever that is, is a tangible thing. Okay. They can be even more tangible. Like we talked about with a print newsletter, like we just did with our dot com secrets labs. That's a physical print, like an actually tangible. They can hold it. Now, if they can't hold it, you have to come back and like, how can I make this thing as tangible as possible and making it like exciting and cool? Okay. Um, the next one here goes to result driven. If you can, if you can tie back to results, you know, how I, um, like, uh, with, with our body evolution one, we took a whole bunch of people through this and most people lost to you 10 and 15 pounds the first week they went on this butter diet. Okay. Uh, we're calling it a skinny bean diet. And so, um, and so our big, our big thing, and again, we're still writing the hook on this, but it's going to be tied to how a small group of, you know, uh, random 26 people who'd never met each other in the world all followed the same diet for 14 days and lost on average 26.2 pounds. Okay. Whatever that is, but it's result driven. Cause they can see that. There's not just some theory, but it's like, here's a result you can get by getting this free CD. Okay. It's very exciting. People love results. And next to me, very specific. The more vague you are, the worst it'll sell the more specific you are to what your system does or what your secret is, whatever it is, the better. And last thing, it's got to be cool. It's got to be something you put out there and people like, oh, I want that. Okay. He's got to pass the, oh, I want that test. Okay. Um, and I would go and I would ask some people that are in your, your market or your business, whatever it is and just ask them, say, Hey, do you think this is cool? Would you want this? And most of them are like, Oh yeah. And they know seriously, I'd rather you hurt my feelings now than make me broke later. Like this is about money. Is this sexy enough for you? Would you actually want this? And they say, no, then, then you need to tweak it a little bit. You got to change it. Okay. Kind of come back to ask campaign that a lot of you guys ran earlier. If you ran that and nobody responded, how do you make that sexier so that people will want that? Okay. Cause until you've got that, that sex appeal, whatever you want to call it, make it sexy enough, the people are going to want it. It's going to be hard to give it away. Okay. Um, but if you make it sexy, man, it'll fly off the shelves. Like literally our dot com stickers, labs book. We try to make it, um, as sexy as we could, 108 proven split test winners. Um, I think you guys have all seen this couple of times. Um, but we gave away in the first like weekend, we gave away almost a thousand of them because it's sexy and it's exciting and it's unique and, and people want to know what this thing is, right? 108 proven split test winners. Boom. That's, that's, uh, let's see if it passes, if it passes all of my tests. So is that a hard offer? Yes. 108 proven split tests to make you more money. Boom. I probably can make it even more hard offer. If I could say, uh, the average people made, you know, $200,000 a year or whatever, but I don't have those numbers. I couldn't do it. Obviously you can't lie on these. Um, so no doubt, but, but, uh, these are all simple tweaks you can make your website to make you more money. So very specific is tangible because it's a physical product. They're getting out. Uh, you guys are mostly guys, we do in CDs or DVDs that helps with the tangible product or tangible thing. It's results driven. These are all proven split tests that we've been running that have made us ton of money. It's specific. It's not just some vague how to make money on the internet. It's split tests that are, you can use specifically for your thing. And is it cool? Yeah. Everyone in our market thinks this cool. Like anyone who does any kind of business wants to sing. That's why it's flying off the shelves. I literally have people on Facebook sharing it with all their friends and their friends passing and the embers is passing along and we don't even have an affiliate program up yet just cause the people think it's cool and they're all sharing it and passing it around. Okay. So the coolness factor is a big part of you guys. You gotta think about that with, uh, with, with the, uh, with the thing you're creating. Okay. All right. Now I want to shift into your story. Um, I initially was going to talk more about this during the copywriting sessions, but, um, one of the, the really common things that, that had a lot of you guys tell me about when you were starting on your CD or your DVD was you had a secret and you're like, okay, here's my secret. You tell it and then it kind of like now what I just told him and that's kind of the whole thing. Excuse me. So why do you say, how do I bulk that up? How do I make it? How do I make those CDs exciting? So it inspires them to want to do more and start thinking about it. I looked back at some of the free offers we do and they all followed this, uh, this process right here. Now this is a, a story selling process. I did not develop this. This was developed by, uh, by one of my, uh, friends and, uh, mentors. His name is Dagen Smith. And Dagen became, uh, a fanatic about story selling, uh, a couple of years ago and he ended up reading like a thousand books on the topic. And then he, he tried and tested thing after thing after thing. And, and the result of all these tests and studies is he created this kind of this map for somebody to tell a story, uh, in a process to sell somebody something at the end of it. And if you look at, if you come on any of my webinars, when I'm selling something, you'll notice I follow this process. If you check out any of my sales letters I'm writing, you'll notice I kind of follow this process. You check out the email sequences I send you. I'm kind of following this process. Okay. Um, if you look at most of my free offers I'm sending you, especially if it's like an audio or video, I'm kind of following this process. Okay. Because sharing the idea is like, is one thing. A lot of times I can show that idea in like five seconds. Okay. I told you guys last week about Evan Pagan, uh, Evan had the, uh, the double your dating offer and his, his big free thing at the front was the kiss test and the kiss test was literally, uh, like two sentences where it said, if you want to find out if a girl is going to kiss you, you, you put her hair, you reach up and you put her hair behind her ear and she leans towards you. You mean she wants you to kiss her. If she doesn't, it means she doesn't want you to. Boom. Now that's the, that's the test, right? I'm going to make a free CD out of that test. Like that's, that's, um, you know, I'm going to cover that in like three seconds and put some of the CD in the list for three seconds and it'll be done. Like what? Like, how does that going to help me? You know, why do I want more? How, you know, it's not, it's not that inspiring. So what I would do if I was Edmond and I was going through this process, I would take a step back and I would tell the story about how I discovered how I learned that secret. Cause if you can do that and you help, you help give someone the same epiphany that you had, um, then they will be yours forever. Okay. I remember Dagan talked about that. He said that the, his entire goal of this story is to, is to share, um, his epiphany in a way where the person listening to it has the same epiphany. And when they have that epiphany with you, um, you don't have to sell them anything. That person now is, uh, they think that what they, they, they basically think that what they're, they're, uh, the, the idea, whatever it is, that it was their idea, cause they had the epiphany. Okay. So here's the process you take someone through. Um, I will put this as a handout inside the members area, so you can print it out as well. I literally have this printed out sitting on my desk right now. I look at it every single day, um, because I use it again in all communication I have with my audience. I'm following this process in some way or the other. Okay. So, um, so I will, uh, I will, uh, walk you guys through this. Okay. So it starts back here. Now, again, this is all about storytelling or story selling. Now most people, um, when they tell stories, they, they do it wrong. They just kind of jump right into the story. Like, okay, so there I was and bubble on. They kind of, they kind of go through this story. If you want to craft a story correctly, you got to tweak and kind of change the, um, you got to change the process of how you, you do it. Okay. Cause you got to capture someone's attention and their imagination quick and fast. Otherwise you'll lose their attention. Right. We all have a million messages coming out every second. If we don't grab your attention and capture it very, very quickly, we're going to lose you. Right. And so when you start a story, you don't start in the middle of it. Um, you don't start or excuse me, you don't even start at the beginning of it. You start at the point of high drama. Okay. Which is usually into the, into the story little ways. You notice that most movies do this. Okay. If they start with like the, the, the, they start at the beginning of the story. It's kind of a boring story. Typically it takes a while to get into the meat and get people excited. Right. So they normally start the movie at a point of high drama. Boom. And then they come back here to the backstory. Okay. But it starts with a point of high drama. All right. Um, probably the best example I can think of this, this is an old movie. I saw it back when I was in high school, but it was a movie called clip hanger with, um, with Sylvester Stallone. And I remember this to this day because I wasn't going to watch the movie. I was at my friend's house. They put it in and I was about to go home and they click play. And as soon as the movie start, uh, boom, they're like, there, there's this whole group of people and they're about to cross this huge ravine. They just strung the, the line across and they're climbing across it. And, uh, the first person gets through, second person gets through and third person, like their harness starts breaking and the person starts slipping. And Sylvester Stallone jumps on the line, runs out there, trying to save the screw. He's holding on her hand and, and her thing breaks and big class pens. He's holding her over this huge ravine. And you think that maybe he's going to save her. And then all of a sudden their hands start slipping and slipping. And then boom, she falls to her death. And I remember watching that. I was like, what in the world just happened in my hands? Like my, just telling the story and thinking right now, my hands are sweating again, my hands were sweating and then boom, it cuts to like this black screen. And you're like, what in the world? And then boom, it comes back and it starts telling the back story. Okay. But now they had me like, I wasn't leaving. I sat down and I had to see the entire movie to find out how everything got to this point. Okay. So think about the movies you watch. You guys, most of them start in a point of high drama, very few start at the beginning, most start at the point of high drama, same thing with your story. Okay. So however you figured out your big secret, like if I was Evan talking about my big secret, I wouldn't talk about, so there I was, is trying to figure out how to kiss a girl. I would start my story with, you know, I was 17 years old. I had braces on. I was scared out of my mind. I was sitting there in the doorstep and I leaned in for a kiss and as soon as I did, boom, I got a slap or she pulled away, whatever it is. Whenever that point was where he felt the most pain telling that part of the story. And then boom, moving back to, you know, five years ago was the first time I went on a date and I was trying to learn this thing and blah, blah, blah, blah. Okay. But it all starts with your high drama. So think about however you discovered whatever it is that you know, what was your point of high drama in your story? Okay. That's where you're going to start your CD or start your DVD. You're going to start telling that part of the story first. Okay. Um, let's say you're in the weight loss space, probably a point where you were super overweight or you had a client that was overweight or you had a heart attack or whatever it is. If you're in the stock trading market, it's probably when you lost all your money or you were broke, um, or, um, one of my friends, he's in the internet marketing space and he told his story about the day when he walked in and his wife was crying and she looked, he looked up and she was holding this letter in his hand and it was a bill she had gotten from AmEx for like $50,000. And she realized that they were, you know, half, you know, 50, there were like two years worth of their salary in the hole because he'd been blowing money on internet marketing stuff and he lied to his wife and now he had to like, you know, redeem himself, you know, but think about like your story. Like what was that point of high drama? The most interesting part of your story, that's what you got to lead with. Okay. Now after you led with that, that point of high drama and kind of explain something, now you come back to your backstory and you start filling in the details. Okay. This is where you tell your story. All of you guys have a story about how you got to where you are. Right. So boom, you're telling that story. And then through the story, you have to tell, you're telling the story about how you discovered the secret, how you discovered the system, whatever it is. Right. And so somewhere in this process, you had an epiphany. Okay. So for me, like, um, I've told the story a lot, um, with, uh, how I learned about email marketing. Okay. My point of high drama is when I got a phone call from my ISP, telling me they were going to sue me for spamming and they disconnected my internet and I had to look at my wife and explain to her why someone was yelling me on the phone and why, you know, we just moved to this brand new house and I was about to get sued by an ISP for spamming. Uh, you know, that's my point of high drama. And then I walked back, you know, I, I learned about list building and I wanted to build a list and, and I tried to tell the whole story of what I was trying to do and, and all this kind of stuff. And then through this story about list building, I share how I had my epiphany about building a list. Why that was so important. Why that was the most important thing I could focus on the entire world. And I share, I don't share it. Here's my epiphany. I share the story about how I got my epiphany. Cause again, if I say, if I say, Hey, I had an epiphany, I needed to build a list. You heard that. And you did. There's not much emotional impact, but if I tell you a story and I, and I, and I just talk about how I learned that. So I talk about how, um, you know, when I was, when I was a kid, I was learning about direct mail and, um, I had the people sending me these, these sales letters in the mail. I remember reading one of them and the guy talked about how, um, how direct mail was easy. All you had to do is you bought a list of a hundred thousand people that had a product here and you know, they were interested in the same topic you were. You write them a sales letter and you send it out. And if just 1% of those people buy, then, then boom, you, you could be rich. You know, 1% of a hundred thousand people buy a $50 product. You just made $50,000. And then I thought that was really cool. And then I got on the internet and realized it was the exact same thing. If I had an email list of a hundred thousand people or a million people, what if I send an email out and just 1% of people bought or just 0.1% or just 0.001%. In fact, if just 0.001% bought a $50 product at, you know, to a hundred thousand person list, that would have been $5,000 for sending out one email. I was more money that I'd seen my entire life. And that's when I had my big epiphany that I needed a list. Okay. But guess what? They had that epiphany with me before I ever said my epiphany, right? Cause I'm telling them about this process and how I discovered it and boom, now they see that they had that same epiphany. Like, wow, I wish I had a list. I'd mail it every day. Not just once a week cause I'd make so much money. Right. They're sharing that epiphany with me. Okay. Now, now in the story, move on to. So after I had that, I, after I had that epiphany, I had this idea explosion. Cause if I had a list, I could, I could create my own products and sell them. And I could sell other people's products and I could become an affiliate and I could do this and this and all these ideas that come to my head. Cause I want them to have the same idea explosion as well. I want them to, to start seeing the possibilities. And if they have the secret I gave them, what else would be possible in their life? What are all the things that are, that are possible? Okay. But in any story, after you have this big idea explosion, then you come to spot, what's kind of your, your point of no return. Like I could do all of this, but man, it's going to cost me a lot of money or it's going to take me a lot of effort. I'm going to have to, you know, learn some stuff or get out of my comfort zone. I have to go talk to girls or I'm going to have to invest in this program or whatever, whatever your point of no return was. Right. So the point of no return and you decided to say yes. Right. And boom, after you decided to say yes, you started building your first list. You asked your first girl out on a date. You decided to, to cut out carbs and start eating butter, whatever your, whatever it was and boom, something happened. Right. Some kind of success came to you. Someone joined your list. You made a dollar. You lost five pounds. You, um, did your first successful trade. You had some kind of small sign of success. Okay. And if you share that story with them, they're going to be like, man, he's had success. I'm going to have success too. Cause they, you're taking them on this journey. Right. All right. So there's the first sign of success. Then you start moving over to, to rapid growth. Okay. My little success started becoming bigger and bigger and all these things started happening. Okay. Now you transition from, from not only did it, this success happened to me, but Hey, I share it with some of my friends and check out what happened to them. Boom. You start sharing case studies. So Susie tried my method and she went out there and she got a boyfriend or she lost weight or she made some money. And then I showed Mike and he did the same thing and he, he, you know, here's his success stories and here's this person and you share success stories because if you just, if the story is only about you, then guess what happens. Everyone was like, well, you're Superman. So of course you can do it. Okay. There's a specific reason why when we sold you guys in the black box funnel, I didn't share very much about my success with black box stuff. I shared Britney's and I shared a whole bunch of other people's. Okay. Vincent's and a bunch of other people because I wanted you to see that this it wasn't just me. Okay. Russell may have superpowers, but look at these other people just like you, they were able to do it. So case studies are important. Now I've shared the case studies. What's cool is to come back and start sharing hidden benefits. Some people will say, yeah, I got a list. I can make money. Boom. But what other benefits come from that? Okay. What other benefits come from losing, losing 30 pounds? What other benefits come from, from learning how to invest? Okay. With make money things, some other benefits that man, now I can donate money to charity. Hey, now I can go buy a new car. Hey, now my friends don't make fun of me anymore. Okay. To lose weight, hidden benefits. Yeah. I lost some weight, but man, I was able to walk and I had more energy now. Okay. I didn't get winded walking up the stairs anymore. People didn't make fun of me anymore. People respected me more. Okay. Investing can be the same kind of thing. So what are the other, the other benefits, the other hidden benefits that come to them because they apply this epiphany that you're the secret, the system that you're giving them. Right. Okay. Now you move over here to the objection transformation. Okay. We all know that our audience can have some objections. Okay. Russell, this is going to be way too hard. Russell, uh, you know, there's no way I'm eating butter. Russell, you know, whatever, whatever the objections you know, your audience is going to have and you got to find those objections and transform them. Okay. A lot of people told me that they would never be able to do this because it didn't cost too much money, but the reality is when I did it, this is what happened. Boom. Okay. Um, the, so that's kind of what I, what I do a lot of times is like a lot of people think, you know, consider this an objection. A lot of people were concerned because they didn't think this was going to work because of this, but the reality is, boom, you kind of lay on them. What, what happened either to you or a case study or, or anybody else or just what the reality is. Okay. Like for example, this weekend I was talking to, to that group that I was with about supplements and all of them were like, Oh, that's cool. But how do you deal with the FDA and how do you, you know, how much, how do you invest in that much product and all the, all these objections that were just funny. And so I went through it. I just, um, transformed their objections. Hey, you know, that's really not objection because of this, you know, that's really not that big a deal because of this. And at the end they're like, wow, we all want our own supplements too. Yeah. Of course you do. Cause I, I, I transferred your objections into things that made you happy. Okay. And so that's the next step in this story selling funnel. Okay. Now you can not hear and you allude to the ultimate twist. Okay. Now all that stuff is cool. While, while this, uh, the secret I showed you is cool. There's actually more and I'm, I can talk to you about here in a minute. I can't tell you about it yet, but there's actually more to this. Okay. This is me setting a hook for what it is I'm going to be selling or inviting them to at the end. Okay. So I'm alluding to this ultimate twist. All right. Um, but before I tell you to talk about that, I want to talk about some of the mistakes I made. Okay. Again, this makes you more human. First mistake I made is I thought I had invested a ton of money. Turns out I didn't. Second mistake I made is, uh, I tried to do this, but you know, it turns out I, I didn't have to. You talk about these mistakes you had, but you were able to get rid of it. Okay. Um, the other part of the mistakes that's powerful as you talk about, like, let's say you're, you're in the forex market, like, um, you know, the, the originally, the first time I taught this, I taught people this, but the problem is a lot of my students weren't successful because of this. So we got rid of those, you know, we eliminated that mistake and we, we found out this problem over here, people are struggling with, we eliminated that mistake. We found this problem, we eliminate it until we eventually were left with the perfect ultimate solution. Okay. So you talk about the mistakes that were in your system or in, in the thing you were doing, how you eliminate them until you create the ultimate solution. Okay. The ultimate solution is what your system is. And then the twist kind of comes back here to what you alluded to earlier. Okay. I told you guys earlier that I, that I stumbled upon the ultimate system. Now, what you didn't know is that, um, boom, you know, for those, you're doing the invisible funnel, we didn't know is I'm actually doing a training on this. Um, and I'm going to spend four hours going through this. It's going to walk you through all the pieces of the, of the puzzle. It's going to eliminate all the mistakes for you. So you can see exactly, uh, how I was able to fill in the blank, lose weight, make money, um, you know, invest my, my retirement income, eat more healthy food, become more natural, whatever it is, but you're looting this ultimate twist. And the twist is kind of like, what kind of ties back into your offer. Like this is the first time and the only time that you're going to be able to discover this entire system once and for all. They shift over here to the pain of an action. You know, if you don't act now these, you know, in a year from now, what's going to be happening, you know, you'll probably still be eating the same thing. You probably still look the same. You'll probably still be trying to build a list and you're not going to have one. Whatever it might be. So because of that, I care about you guys. I want to invite you and invite you to come and join me on this webinar. Join me on this invisible funnel. Join me for my coaching program. Join me on my, whatever, whatever you're inviting them to do at the end. Okay. And there it is. Yes. That's the, the story selling process. That pretty cool. Now some of you guys were asking, um, Steve asked if I'm recording this. Yes, Steve, I'm recording it. A couple of questions I saw while I was going through this, um, people were saying Russell, should I use this on my free CD? I use it during my invisible funnel webinar. So I'm using it. Where should I be using this? The answer is yes to all of those. Okay. If you guys notice, if you're, if you've been following my marketing for a while, you notice I tell stories a lot, usually the same stories and using different formats on a webinar, I might tell a story and then I might tell it differently through a, through an email series. I might tell different, but I'm telling similar stories that have happened to me. All going through the same process. Okay. And so if you got a free CD and you're talking about, um, you know, and you're talking about a secret, your epiphany or your system, which is your epiphany, I would, this is how I would introduce it. Okay. I spend, I spend 90% of my time going through the story to get them excited and fired up, maybe 10% of my time explaining the secret or explaining here's the system. Okay. Step one of the systems, you've got to invest money here. Step two in the system is this step three, cause you don't, your, your goal, the free CD is not to give them all things. Your goal, the free CD or the free DVD is to be useful, but incomplete and to get them fired up and motivated to be cut, to take action and to learn more from you. Okay. And so this, the story selling process will get them engaged and excited more so than you just teaching for, for 90 minutes. Okay. Or 60 minutes, whatever it might be. In fact, it's kind of interesting. Um, when I first started doing, uh, when I first, well, kind of the back story, let me, let me step back to my backstory here. Actually, so I started at the point of high drama. So, uh, the point of high drama, like I was sitting in an event room and I saw this guy speaking and he started closing sale at the end of his, and I was there thinking I was learning, I was going to learn internet marketing. And at the end of his speech, he told everyone to run the back and to buy his course for $2,000. I remember looking back in the back of the room and I saw him boom, the first person go back and, uh, and just like really run the back and buy. And then second person and third, and so looking back there and counting how many people were back there. I realized that, that from that guy's 90 minute presentation, he made like $75,000 in sales. And then second speaker got up to the exact same thing. And I remember watching people run to the back and throw their credit cards down. But this time this guy's package was $5,000 and he made 150,000. And after watching this for three entire days, I realized I need to become a, uh, a stage presenter. Okay. There's my high drama. And I come back now prior to this, I'd been an internet marketing nerd. I just been selling stuff online. I'd never had any people skills. I was scared to death to talk to anybody until that day when I had the epiphany, and I saw the power of speaking in front of an audience, how you could close not, not just sell one person at a time, you can sell 20 or 50 or a hundred people at a time. And that's when I made my big epiphany that I wanted to be a stage presenter. Okay. And I keep going through this whole process. If I was, if I was doing it, but that's, but anyway, so I'm telling that story because when I first learned stage, stage presenting, I went, I got hardcore to, I bought everyone's training on the topic. I tried to learn it. And then, uh, and then I started going to seminars and speaking. I remember I would go and I was, and I would watch the other speakers speak. And I was like, and I see them sell and make, you know, 100, 100 grand, 200 grand, 300 grand from a 90 minute presentation. And listen, I'm like, these guys are horrible. Like I know way more stuff than I can teach way better stuff than they do. So I get there, I would teach and sure enough, I would teach way better stuff than they ever did. So much better stuff. But guess what happened? I would close at the end and nobody bought. And I was so confused. And I kept going through the, this public speaking training and finally one guy's training really spoke to me. He said, look, he's like your job of, of a speaker, if you're trying to sell something that was different, if you're trying to, to provide value and to teach somebody, right? Okay, now I'm just teaching you guys, I'm not selling you anything. But if I was trying to sell you something, the worst thing I could possibly do is to just teach you everything. Cause then there's no, there's no purpose for you to buy. Okay. It's useful, but incomplete. And so he said, basically the core of it, he said, the core of, of a stage presentation is you got to tell stories about kind of this whole process. You tell a story about how you learned what you learned. You share results about other people who did it and then you close them. And so against my, my, uh, my belief patterns against everything I wanted to test this guy out. So I tweaked my presentation. I just told my story, shared results and closed at the end. And that was the very first time I saw a table rush. People run the back and invest money and buy my stuff. Okay. This story process, you guys will inspire people. You'll get them to want to listen to you and it'll get them to buy from you. Okay. More so than giving them the best content in the world you have. You're going to give them stuff. You're, you promise them you're going to give them a secret or your system. You're going to give them that through this, through this process, but more so, you're going to inspire them because you want them to receive some value, but you want them to get into your value ladder and boom, to now take the invisible funnel and then eventually you're coaching and so on and so forth.
